seo.title: Invoicemonk vs Wave (2026): Best Wave Alternative for International Freelancers
seo.description: Wave dropped support for users outside the US and Canada in November 2020. Invoicemonk is the cross-border alternative — compliant invoicing, multi-currency, and automatic reminders that work in every country.

hero.title: Invoicemonk vs Wave (2026): Which Is Better for International Freelancers?
hero.subtitle: Quick answer: Wave is a solid free accounting tool — but it officially stopped supporting users outside the United States and Canada in November 2020. If you bill international clients from anywhere else, Wave cannot send your invoices or process your payments. Invoicemonk is built specifically for cross-border freelance invoicing — compliance-first invoices, multi-currency, automatic reminders, and payment tracking in any country.

whoFor.title: Who this comparison is for
whoFor.body: This guide is written for freelancers — developers, designers, growth consultants, copywriters, and other independent professionals — who invoice clients in a different country or currency. If you are based in Nigeria, Kenya, the Philippines, India, the UK, Germany, or anywhere outside the US and Canada, and your clients pay in USD, EUR, or GBP, this page explains exactly how Invoicemonk and Wave compare on the things that matter most: international compliance, multi-currency invoicing, payment reminders, and what happens when something goes wrong.

keyFact.title: The most important fact: Wave no longer supports international users
keyFact.intro: Before comparing features, pricing, or design, there is one fact that overrides everything else.
keyFact.body: In November 2020, Wave officially announced it was discontinuing support for all users outside the United States and Canada. According to Wave's own Help Center, users outside these two countries "can no longer send invoices or reminders through Wave" and "compliance could not be guaranteed outside the supported regions." Wave directed affected international users to switch to Zoho Books.
keyFact.examplesTitle: This is not a minor limitation. It means:
examples: If you are a freelance developer in Lagos billing a client in San Francisco, Wave will not send your invoice.
If you are a designer in Nairobi receiving payments in USD, Wave cannot process your payment.
If you are a growth consultant in Manila chasing an overdue invoice, Wave's automated reminder system will not work for you.
keyFact.closing: Wave's existing accounts outside the US and Canada remain technically accessible for basic bookkeeping, but Wave itself states this may change. Any freelancer outside North America building their invoicing workflow on Wave is building on an unstable foundation. Invoicemonk was built for exactly the freelancers Wave abandoned — its entire product is designed around cross-border freelance work.

overview: Geography is the deciding factor. Wave restricted new signups to the US and Canada and supports invoice sending only in those two countries. Invoicemonk works in every country with internet access and includes multi-currency on every plan — Starter (free, 5 invoices/mo), Pro ($15/mo, unlimited), SME ($49/mo, unlimited users) and Biz (custom, e-invoicing & government submission).

Compliance is the second gap. Every Invoicemonk invoice is hashed into an immutable audit trail and carries a QR code anyone can scan to verify authenticity — features that ship on the free Starter plan. Wave does not offer credit notes, an immutable audit log, or invoice verification.

features.name: E-invoicing mandates supported
features.invoicemonk: 16 jurisdictions (ZATCA Ph1/Ph2, MyInvois, GST IRN, Peppol EU, FIRS MBS, MTD UK, KRA eTIMS, GRA e-VAT, BIR CAS, SARS, ZIMRA, SRC, SEF Brazil, US B2G, CRA+Peppol)

features.name: Available to users outside US & Canada

sections.title: Compliance and cross-border invoicing
body: A compliant invoice for international work is not just a PDF with your name and a number on it. Depending on where you are based and where your client is located, a legally valid invoice may need your tax identification number, the client's VAT or company registration number, the correct currency and exchange rate notation, a specific date format, payment terms that meet local requirements, and jurisdiction-specific line items for tax treatment.

Invoicemonk is built around this reality. It generates invoices that meet the requirements of the specific countries involved in the transaction — not a generic template that may or may not hold up when your client's finance team processes it. Wave's invoicing was designed for North American small businesses operating in a single currency under the US or Canadian tax code; the compliance layer simply does not exist for cross-border transactions.

sections.title: Automatic payment reminders
body: Getting paid on time is the single biggest cash flow problem for freelancers. Research consistently shows that a significant proportion of freelance invoices are paid late, and that automated reminders reduce average payment times substantially.

Invoicemonk's auto-reminder system lets you set reminders to fire automatically before and after an invoice's due date — without you having to chase each client. Wave does offer automated reminders, but only for users in the US and Canada who have activated Wave's online payments feature. For the vast majority of international freelancers, the reminder feature does not function.

sections.title: Multi-currency support
body: Invoicemonk supports invoicing in multiple currencies, allowing you to bill US clients in USD, European clients in EUR, and UK clients in GBP — all from a single account. Exchange rates are handled automatically.

Wave records foreign currency transactions in your base business currency for bookkeeping using xe.com rates. That is a bookkeeping feature, not an invoicing feature — it tracks what you received, it does not help you send a compliant multi-currency invoice to an international client.

wave.title: Wave pricing (2026)

tiers.name: Starter — Free
items: Unlimited invoices and estimates
Basic accounting and expense tracking
Bank reconciliation (manual)
Income and expense reports
No invoice sending for users outside US/Canada
No automated payment reminders outside US/Canada
No human support (chatbot only)

tiers.name: Pro — $16/month
items: Everything in Starter
Automated bank transaction imports
Unlimited receipt capture
Priority support via live chat and email
Remove Wave branding from invoices
Pro features still geographically restricted for international users

addOns: Credit card processing: 2.9% + $0.60 per transaction (3.4% + $0.60 for Amex)
ACH bank payments: 1% per transaction ($1 minimum)
wave.trueCost: The true cost of Wave's "free" plan: while the Starter tier has no monthly fee, transaction fees on payments processed through Wave add up quickly. A freelancer processing $5,000/month through Wave's payment system pays roughly $145–$170 in fees per month — about $1,740–$2,040 per year on a plan marketed as free.

invoicemonk.title: Invoicemonk pricing (2026)
invoicemonk.intro: Invoicemonk is designed for freelancers doing cross-border work, with pricing that reflects the full scope of what international freelancers need — compliance-first invoicing, multi-currency, automated reminders, payment tracking — available in every country from day one.

tiers.name: Pro
tiers.desc: Unlimited invoices, multi-currency, up to 5 team members, custom branding.

tiers.name: SME
tiers.desc: Online payments, advanced reports, premium templates, unlimited team.

tiers.name: Biz
tiers.desc: E-invoicing & government submission, dedicated manager, SLA.

rows.name: E-invoicing mandates supported
values: 16 jurisdictions (ZATCA Ph1/Ph2, MyInvois, GST IRN, Peppol EU, FIRS MBS, MTD UK, KRA eTIMS, GRA e-VAT, BIR CAS, SARS, ZIMRA, SRC, SEF Brazil, US B2G, CRA+Peppol)

wave.title: Use Wave if…
wave.intro: Wave is a genuinely good product for one specific use case: a small business or sole trader based in the United States or Canada, billing domestic clients, wanting free basic accounting and invoicing software.
items: You are based in the US or Canada
You bill domestic clients in USD or CAD
You don't need cross-border compliance
You don't need invoices that comply outside the US/Canadian tax framework
You're happy with chatbot-only support on the Pro plan

invoicemonk.title: Use Invoicemonk if…
invoicemonk.intro: Invoicemonk is built for the freelancer that Wave explicitly stopped serving: independent professionals anywhere in the world who bill clients across borders.
items: You are a freelance developer, designer, writer, consultant, or growth manager
You invoice US, UK, EU, or other clients from a different country
You need invoices that meet the requirements of the countries involved
You want automatic reminders that actually fire — without manual chasing
You need to invoice in multiple currencies from a single account
You want a dashboard that tells you what's paid, overdue, and needs follow-up

scenario.title: How each tool handles a real cross-border invoice
scenario.intro: Scenario: you are a freelance developer based in Lagos. Your client is a startup in San Francisco. You have completed a project worth $3,500. You need to send a compliant invoice, receive payment, and follow up if payment is late.

wave.title: With Wave
steps: You log into Wave and try to send your invoice.
Wave's platform blocks invoice sending — your account is outside the US and Canada.
You can export the invoice as a PDF and email it manually.
There is no automated reminder if it goes overdue. You must chase the client yourself.
Wave's support team cannot assist you — support for your region was discontinued.

invoicemonk.title: With Invoicemonk
steps: You create an invoice in Invoicemonk. The platform generates a compliant invoice that meets the requirements for a US-based client receiving services from a Nigerian freelancer.
You send the invoice directly through Invoicemonk.
You set an automatic reminder to fire 3 days before the due date, and again 7 days after if unpaid.
Your client receives a professional invoice and a gentle reminder at the right time.
You track payment status in your dashboard — no spreadsheet required.
When you have a question, support is available to you.

socialProof.title: What freelancers say about Wave's international limitations
socialProof.body: User feedback on Wave's international restrictions is consistent across review platforms. Reviewers on GetApp, Capterra, and NerdWallet note that Wave's customer support issues have caused many users to switch products altogether — with one of the most cited reasons being the inability to get human support without paying for services that international users cannot access regardless. NerdWallet's April 2025 review notes Wave has moved many features to its paid plan in recent years and that an "overwhelming number of Wave reviewers criticize its customer support." For international freelancers, the situation is more straightforward: the core functionality — sending invoices and receiving payments — simply does not work.

migration.title: How do I migrate from Wave to Invoicemonk?

steps.title: Export your Wave data
steps.desc: Wave → Settings → Data Export. Download customers, invoices and transactions as CSV. International users still have read access for export.

steps.title: Create your Invoicemonk account
steps.desc: Create your account at app.invoicemonk.com. Add business profile, logo and tax ID in under 5 minutes.

steps.title: Import customers and history
steps.desc: Upload the Wave CSVs. Names, emails, addresses and invoice history map automatically.

steps.title: Set up multi-currency accounts
steps.desc: Add a currency account for every market you invoice — EUR, GBP, INR and more — included on every plan.

steps.title: Send a globally-compliant invoice
steps.desc: Your first Invoicemonk invoice is hashed, QR-verifiable, and ready for any tax authority.

items.question: Can I use Wave as a freelancer outside the US and Canada?
items.answer: You can access your Wave account and do manual bookkeeping, but Wave cannot send invoices, process payments, or send payment reminders on your behalf if your business is registered outside the United States or Canada. Wave officially discontinued support for international users in November 2020.

items.question: Does Invoicemonk work for freelancers in Africa, Asia, or Europe?
items.answer: Yes. Invoicemonk is available to freelancers in all countries and is specifically designed for cross-border invoicing — billing clients in a different country from where you are based.

items.question: Does Wave handle VAT or foreign tax compliance?
items.answer: Wave was built for the US and Canadian tax frameworks. It does not provide compliance guidance or invoice generation for VAT jurisdictions, GST, or other international tax requirements.

items.question: Is Wave free forever?
items.answer: Wave's Starter plan has no monthly subscription fee, but it charges transaction fees (2.9% + $0.60 per transaction for card payments; 1% minimum $1 for ACH). For international users, even the Pro plan is functionally limited to basic bookkeeping.

items.question: Can Invoicemonk replace Wave for bookkeeping?
items.answer: Invoicemonk is focused on the invoicing and payment-tracking workflow that matters most to freelancers. If you need deep double-entry accounting, Wave's bookkeeping features — or a dedicated accounting tool — may serve that need alongside Invoicemonk.

items.question: What should Wave international users switch to?
items.answer: Wave itself recommends Zoho Books for users outside the US and Canada. Invoicemonk is a stronger option specifically for freelancers doing cross-border work, because it is built around the compliance and invoicing requirements of international freelance — not just general small business accounting.

bottomLine.title: The bottom line
body: Wave is a capable free accounting tool for North American small businesses. For international freelancers — the developers, designers, writers, and consultants doing the majority of their work across borders — Wave made a deliberate decision to stop serving you in 2020.

Invoicemonk was built to serve exactly this market. It handles the compliance, the multi-currency invoicing, the automated reminders, and the payment tracking that freelancers doing cross-border work depend on every day. If you are a freelancer anywhere in the world billing international clients, Invoicemonk is built for you. Wave is not.
